title: Cross-platform release and installer hardening
description: Prepare releases after core functionality is real. Scope: Windows/macOS/Linux CI, wasm CI, cargo-dist or equivalent binary artifacts, checksums, install docs, changelog discipline, versioned capabilities contract, release smoke tests, and no untracked generated artifacts.

title: Ship first-class browser/WASM package and demo
description: Expose the render core to browser/WASM as a first-class product surface. Scope: wasm feature/package, JS/TS-friendly API for render_html/render_pdf, font/style bytes input, no filesystem/process/runtime assumptions, browser demo page (textarea -> preview -> PDF download), wasm size budget, browser tests, and deterministic wasm output fixtures.

title: Build full CommonMark/GFM conformance ladder
description: Move the current useful parser subset to a rigorous conformance track. Scope: source spans, setext headings, reference links, lazy continuations, nested list edge cases, HTML block policy, full CommonMark fixture harness, GFM tables/tasks/strike/autolinks, malformed input tests, and parser fuzzing. Do not pull comrak/pulldown-cmark into production; they may be used only as read-only/differential reference tools in dev gates if approved.

title: Implement PDF block and page layout
description: Build document/page layout on top of shaped text and line breaking. Scope: page boxes/margins, heading keep-with-next, widow/orphan control, list and blockquote spacing, code block layout, table column measurement, table page breaks, repeated table headers, image sizing, headers/footers if needed, and deterministic pagination fixtures.

title: Quality gate: prove deterministic CLI and HTML output
description: Add a fast local/CI script that proves stable bytes for agent-facing JSON surfaces and HTML rendering. Scope: render the same Markdown twice, compare HTML bytes, compare capabilities/doctor/robot-triage JSON bytes, avoid shared Cargo target-dir contention, document the gate, and keep it independent from PDF until PDF bytes exist.
close_reason: Added deterministic-output gate for current stable surfaces. scripts/check-determinism.sh compares capabilities --json, doctor --json, --robot-triage, HTML stdout, and HTML file bytes across repeated runs using repo-local target/fmd-checks artifacts. CI now runs it; README/plan/changelog document it. Proof: bash -n scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; git diff --check on determinism-gate paths. Full test/clippy gate is currently blocked by a separate uncommitted PDF implementation lane that changes the PDF CLI contract and is being coordinated over Agent Mail.

title: Design config and theme persistence for fmd without hurting WASM
description: Add native CLI configuration only after the core options settle. Scope: fmd config get/set/show --json, default font family, page size/margins, theme choice, custom CSS defaults, XDG/platform config paths, explicit no-config mode for reproducibility, and equivalent WASM option structs that do not read local files.
close_reason: Implemented native fmd config persistence without touching the WASM/core path: std-only key=value config, FMD_CONFIG/XDG/platform path resolution, config show/get/set/path --json, persistent font/custom-CSS/dark-mode/page-margin settings, render integration, and --no-config reproducibility switch. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo check --no-default-features --lib; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh. Note: src/text.rs is a peer dirty font-lane file and was not included.

title: Implement clean-room syntax highlighting for documentation code blocks
description: Create a small deterministic syntax highlighter instead of depending on syntect. Start with documentation-heavy languages: Rust, Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, Bash, JSON, TOML, YAML, SQL, HTML/CSS, and Markdown. Emit shared token streams consumed by HTML and PDF. Unknown languages fall back to plain code. Include golden token/output fixtures and performance tests.

title: Implement Knuth-Plass paragraph breaking and hyphenation
description: Build the LaTeX-grade paragraph engine: boxes/glue/penalties, demerit optimization, badness tuning, kerning-aware line widths, Liang/TeX hyphenation patterns, optional justification, ragged setting, leading/baseline rules, and microtypographic protrusion experiments. Include fixtures measuring line breaks, hyphen counts, raggedness, and deterministic output.

title: Implement compact deterministic PDF writer
description: Write our own minimal PDF backend for this renderer. Scope: deterministic object numbering, catalog/pages/content/font objects, text operators over shaped glyph runs, embedded subset fonts, FlateDecode streams, metadata controlled by options/SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH, stable output bytes, structural validation, file-size benchmarks, and tiny fixture PDFs for primitive blocks.

title: Land PDF MVP CLI contract and determinism gate
description: Align the project around the first working clean-room PDF writer without pretending the full typography engine is done. Scope: keep the deterministic Base-14 PDF v0, update fmd doctor/capabilities/robot-docs/--robot-triage from PDF refusal to available_v0_base14, replace obsolete exit-70 tests with PDF success/structure tests, extend determinism checking to PDF bytes, update README/CHANGELOG/plan, and leave Knuth-Plass/font-subsetting roadmap beads open.
close_reason: Implemented PDF MVP contract alignment: fmd now reports pdf=available_v0_base14, obsolete exit-70 assertions are replaced with success/structure tests, PDF bytes joined the determinism gate, README/CHANGELOG/plan are updated, and the full typography/font-subsetting PDF beads remain open.

title: Parser conformance: indented code blocks
description: Implement top-level four-space indented code blocks, stripping exactly one code indent, preserving code text safely, adding regressions, and keeping list item indentation behavior unchanged.
close_reason: Implemented top-level four-space indented code blocks with one-indent stripping, safe HTML escaping inside code, blank separator handling, and list indentation regression. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo check --no-default-features --lib; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh.

title: Parser conformance: support setext headings
description: Implement CommonMark-style setext headings for simple paragraphs followed by === or --- underline lines. Preserve thematic breaks where the line is not a valid setext underline, add regression tests, and keep HTML heading IDs deterministic.
close_reason: Implemented setext heading parsing and regression coverage. Paragraphs followed by === become h1, paragraphs followed by --- or - become h2, standalone --- remains a thematic break, and indented dash lines are not treated as setext underlines. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo check --no-default-features --lib; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; cargo test (25 tests); cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.

title: Parser conformance: add reference-link definitions
description: Add a reference-definition collection pass and inline resolution for [text][id], [text][], and shortcut references, with malformed-definition tests and no production dependency on external Markdown parsers.
close_reason: Implemented reference-link definitions and resolution. The parser now collects top-level reference definitions with normalized labels and first-definition-wins behavior, removes valid definitions from rendered output, resolves full/collapsed/shortcut reference links plus reference images, and leaves malformed definitions visible as text. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo check --no-default-features --lib; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; cargo test; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.

title: Parser conformance: lazy continuation and nested list fixtures
description: Harden paragraph/list parsing for lazy continuation lines, nested ordered/unordered lists, task-list preservation, and mixed blockquote/list cases using focused regression fixtures.
close_reason: Implemented lazy continuation and nested list parsing. List items are now parsed as nested block streams, unindented lazy continuation lines stay in the current item, nested ordered/unordered lists render as nested lists, task-list continuation text is preserved, and blockquote/list nesting has regression coverage. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo check --no-default-features --lib; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; cargo test; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.

title: Harden security posture for untrusted Markdown
description: Define and test the safety model. Scope: raw HTML disabled by default, URL/image policy, escaping guarantees, no network access during render, no ambient filesystem in core, resource limits for huge documents, fuzzing for parser/layout/PDF writer, panic-free malformed input handling, and CLI errors that do not leak unrelated local data.

title: Parser conformance: fuzz and differential fixture harness
description: Create a parser fuzz/metamorphic harness and a dev-only differential fixture runner against approved reference outputs; no production dependency on comrak/pulldown-cmark.
close_reason: Implemented dependency-free parser conformance harness: scripts/parser-diff.sh, deterministic metamorphic pseudo-fuzz tests, and approved parser fixture snapshots. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo check --no-default-features --lib; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh.

title: Quality gate: enforce clean-room dependency and unsafe policy
description: Add a fast script and CI step that enforce franken_markdown's clean-room policy: core no-default-features builds with no third-party normal dependencies, unsafe code remains forbidden, banned renderer/browser dependency forests stay out of the graph, and the check is documented for local use.
close_reason: Added clean-room policy gate. scripts/check-policy.sh verifies the no-default core has zero third-party normal dependencies, banned renderer/browser/runtime crates are absent from the all-features graph, build.rs is absent, and unsafe-code lint enforcement remains active. CI now runs it; docs/plan/changelog document it. The check scripts default to target/fmd-checks to avoid shared target-dir contention. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo check --no-default-features --lib; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; cargo test (25 tests); cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.

title: Parser conformance: raw HTML block and inline policy
description: Implement a conservative raw HTML parser policy: detect common block HTML starts, inline HTML tags/comments/processing instructions, escape by default, pass through only with allow_raw_html, add security-focused regressions, and keep malformed angle-bracket text safe.
close_reason: Implemented conservative raw HTML parsing policy: common block tags/comments become HtmlBlock, inline tags/comments become Inline::Html, default rendering escapes, allow_raw_html/--allow-html passes trusted HTML through, malformed angle-bracket text stays escaped. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo check --no-default-features --lib; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh. Note: src/text.rs is peer dirty font-lane work and was not included.

title: Make default HTML output visually excellent and snapshot-tested
description: Raise the HTML renderer from good scaffold to excellent default output. Scope: Cursor/GitHub-like typography, tables with subtle striping and responsive overflow, blockquotes, lists, code blocks, images, dark/light behavior, print CSS, custom stylesheet contract, embedded-font CSS path, deterministic formatting, HTML golden snapshots, browser screenshot tests, and accessibility/readability checks.

title: Create visual quality, determinism, and performance gauntlet
description: Build the evidence system for quality claims. Scope: curated Markdown corpus, HTML snapshots, browser screenshots, PDF raster/visual comparisons once available, PDF structural validation, output-size ledger, parse/render benchmarks, compile-time/dependency-size ledger, determinism checks across repeated runs and SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH, and negative evidence for rejected dependencies/optimizations.

title: Parser conformance: source spans and diagnostics scaffold
description: Introduce source-span data structures for block and inline nodes without disrupting renderer APIs; use spans in parser diagnostics and future editor/WASM integrations.
close_reason: Added additive source-span API and parser diagnostics scaffold: SourceSpan/Spanned/SpannedDocument types, parse_markdown_spanned, warning diagnostics for malformed reference definitions and unclosed fences, and regression tests. Existing renderer-facing Document API remains unchanged. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo check --no-default-features --lib; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh.

title: Build clean-room font reader, metrics, shaping, and subsetting
description: Implement the text/font subsystem. Scope: parse required TTF/OTF tables (cmap, head, hhea, hmtx, maxp, name, OS/2, kern, selected GPOS/GSUB), Latin-first glyph mapping, advances, kerning, common ligatures, glyph outline access, deterministic subset generation, bundled/caller-provided font bytes, and fixtures for sans/serif/mono. No system font discovery in core or WASM.

title: Add Asupersync-native batch orchestration outside the render core
description: Use Asupersync where it adds leverage: native batch rendering, cancellation, budgets, child regions, parallel job orchestration, receipts, and deterministic LabRuntime tests. Keep the render core synchronous and embeddable. Owned async APIs must put &Cx first, preserve Outcome until the CLI boundary, checkpoint long loops, and prove cleanup/cancellation with deterministic tests.

title: Design shared theme/style model for HTML, PDF, CLI, and WASM
description: Replace ad-hoc styling with a stable shared model that drives both HTML and PDF: font family, mono family, colors, spacing, table density, code theme, page size, margins, line-height/leading, dark mode policy, and custom CSS replacement for HTML. Make the model serializable enough for CLI JSON/config and WASM callers.
close_reason: Implemented structured dependency-free shared theme model: typed font/mono/color/spacing/table-density/code-theme/dark-mode/page contract, stable hand-rolled JSON for CLI/config/WASM, HTML CSS generated from theme tokens, CLI discovery surfaces updated, and theme tests added. Avoided pdf/text/layout lane. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo check --no-default-features --lib; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; scripts/check-policy.sh; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; scripts/check-determinism.sh; scripts/parser-diff.sh.

title: Harden fmd agent ergonomics and command contract
description: Apply the agent-ergonomics bar to fmd. Required surfaces: fmd <file>, fmd -, fmd --text, render --json, capabilities --json, doctor --json, robot-docs guide, stable exit codes, stdout=data/stderr=diagnostics, useful errors that name exact corrective commands, NO_COLOR/CI/non-TTY discipline, golden tests for help/JSON schemas, and intent inference for common mistakes.
close_reason: Hardened fmd command contract: parse errors now return documented exit 64 with teaching hint, common --json typos normalize before clap parsing, bare fmd prints long first-try help, --robot-triage JSON mega-surface added, README/CHANGELOG updated, and binary-level contract tests now pin fmd <file>, fmd -, --text, discovery JSON, typed PDF refusal, usage errors, typo inference, stdout/stderr split, and NO_COLOR/CI/TERM=dumb behavior. Proof: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo check --no-default-features --lib; cargo test (13 tests); cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; manual jq/smoke checks for --robot-triage, parse-error exit 64, and --jason render recovery.

title: Build franken_markdown into a best-in-class Markdown to HTML/PDF/WASM renderer
description: Umbrella epic for turning the current pre-Phase-0 scaffold into a production-quality clean-room renderer. Goals: Markdown file/stdin/raw text to beautiful all-in-one HTML and tiny optimized PDF; standalone agent-ergonomic fmd CLI; first-class browser/WASM API; dependency-lean memory-safe Rust; no browser/Typst/comrak/syntect/cosmic-text/krilla dependency forest; Asupersync only for native orchestration where it adds cancellation, budgets, structured concurrency, and deterministic tests.

title: Phase 0: lock governance, docs, license, CLI contract, and proof gates
description: Finish repository governance before deeper implementation. Scope: AGENTS.md, README.md, CHANGELOG.md, comprehensive plan, MIT+OpenAI/Anthropic rider, Cargo metadata, fmd capabilities/doctor/robot-docs, stable exit-code contract, first-try commands, no-default-features core build proof, cargo fmt/check/clippy/test gates, and initial bead graph. Success means future agents can understand status and next work without reading the original chat.
close_reason: Completed in this pass: project-local AGENTS/README/CHANGELOG/plan, license rider, Cargo metadata, fmd capabilities/doctor/robot-docs and first-try render shortcuts, no-default-features core proof, cargo fmt/check/clippy/test gates, initial bead graph.

title: Phase 0: prove first-class WASM core boundary
description: Make WASM first-class immediately. Scope: keep the render core independent from CLI/filesystem/process/native runtime assumptions; add a wasm-oriented feature plan; prove cargo check --no-default-features for the core; add CI notes and initial wasm target check plan. This blocks claims that the project is browser-ready until the boundary is proven.
close_reason: Added first-class WASM/core boundary gate without touching the render-core API contract: scripts/check-wasm-core.sh checks cargo check --no-default-features --lib for native and wasm32-unknown-unknown; GitHub Actions CI runs fmt, native checks, no-default core, wasm-core script, tests, clippy, plus macOS/Windows check+test; README and comprehensive plan document the gate. Proof on active tree: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo check --no-default-features --lib; scripts/check-wasm-core.sh; cargo test (20 tests); cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.
